identity.name: AI Ecosystem & Supply-Chain Risk — Vulnerable ML Dependencies (T1195.002)
identity.summary: Cortex Cloud AI-SPM enumerates third-party ML dependencies shipped in a Lambda layer and SageMaker workload, flags known-CVE packages (transformers and tensorflow), annotates each with EU AI Act risk classification, and traces the dependency graph SageMaker -> Lambda -> OpenAI proxy -> external provider. The scenario validates the CVE findings and the dependency-edge chain against the posture f…
identity.description: AI workloads inherit risk from a deep, fast-moving open-source supply chain — model loaders, tokenizers, and serving frameworks with documented RCE and deserialization CVEs. AI Security Posture Management must enumerate those third-party ML dependencies, match them to known CVEs, and trace where a compromised package could reach. This scenario plants a Lambda layer carrying vulnerable ML deps (transformers < 4.30 / CVE-2024-3568 model-loading RCE; tensorflow < 2.13.1 / CVE-2023-25660 saved-model deserialization) wired into a SageMaker -> Lambda -> OpenAI-proxy chain, and validates that Cortex Cloud AI-SPM raises at least two CVE-tagged dependency findings and reconstructs the dependency graph. Detection is posture-oriented: validation queries assert distinct CVE findings on ML dependencies and count the dependency edges in the inventory graph. For EMEA regulated POVs, each finding is annotated with EU AI Act (Article 6 / Annex III) risk classification. A correlation rule fuses the CVE findings with the traced chain into one AI supply-chain risk incident.

techniques.name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain

execution.framework_reference_id: SIM-AISPM-003 — Cortex Cloud AI-SPM supply-chain scan + CVE/dependency-graph probe

prerequisites.description: Cortex Cloud AI-SPM supply-chain scanning enabled against the target AWS account.
ai-spm IaC module applied so the Lambda layer with vulnerable ML deps is planted.

payload.code:
# Posture validation — probe AI-SPM supply-chain CVE findings + dependency graph.
curl -fsSL -H "Authorization: Bearer ${CORTEX_CLOUD_API_TOKEN}" "${CORTEX_CLOUD_API_BASE}/findings?category=ai_supply_chain&tag=CortexSimAISPMFinding" | jq '[.findings[] | select(.cve_id != null)] | length'

expected_artifacts.description: Dependency-edge chain SageMaker -> Lambda -> external provider.

xql_queries.name: AISPM-003 Supply Chain Manifest Capture Bookkeeping
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter cloud_provider = "AWS" and resource_type = "lambda_layer_version"
| filter resource_name contains "vulnerable-ml-deps"
| fields _time, operation_name, resource_name

xql_queries.name: AISPM-003 Supply Chain Scan Discovers ML Dependencies
xql_queries.query:
dataset = ai_spm_findings
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter finding_type = "dependency_discovered"
| filter dependency in ("transformers", "tensorflow")
| comp count() as found by dependency
| fields dependency, found

xql_queries.name: AISPM-003 CVE Tagged ML Dependency Findings
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cortex_cloud_posture
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter finding_type = "vulnerable_dependency" and cve != null
| filter dependency in ("transformers", "tensorflow") and tags contains "CortexSimAISPMFinding"
| comp count_distinct(cve) as unique_cves by dependency
| filter unique_cves >= 1
| fields dependency, cve, unique_cves

xql_queries.name: AISPM-003 Dependency Graph Chain SageMaker Lambda Provider
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cortex_cloud_posture
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter asset_type = "ai_dependency_edge"
| filter ai_service in ("sagemaker", "lambda", "openai_proxy")
| comp count() as edges_in_chain
| filter edges_in_chain >= 2
| fields edges_in_chain

correlation_rules.name: AI Supply Chain Risk — CVE Dependency In Traced AI Chain
correlation_rules.logic: XQL(AISPM-003 CVE Tagged ML Dependency Findings) AND XQL(AISPM-003 Dependency Graph Chain SageMaker Lambda Provider)

analytics_modules: Cortex Cloud AI-SPM — ML dependency CVE enrichment and EU AI Act classification
Cortex Cloud Posture — AI dependency-graph reconstruction

products.evidence_query: dataset = cortex_cloud_posture | filter finding_type = "vulnerable_dependency" | fields _time, dependency, cve, severity, regulatory_tags
Correlation CR-AISPM-0003 fuses CVE-tagged ML-dependency findings with the traced AI dependency chain into one supply-chain risk incident.

use_cases.name: AI Ecosystem & Supply Chain Risk
use_cases.description: Enumerate third-party ML dependencies, flag CVEs, annotate EU AI Act risk, and trace the AI dependency graph.

test_cases.objective: At least two CVE-tagged ML-dependency findings and a >= 3-node dependency chain surface.
preconditions: Cortex Cloud AI-SPM supply-chain scanning enabled.
ai-spm fixture with vulnerable Lambda layer applied.
steps: Observe supply-chain findings and dependency graph in Cortex Cloud.
success_criteria: CVE findings query returns >= 2 distinct CVEs.
Correlation CR-AISPM-0003 raises one supply-chain risk incident.

preventive_controls: Pin and continuously patch ML dependencies; block known-CVE versions in CI.
Vendor and verify model-loading and serving frameworks before deployment.
Gate third-party LLM provider integrations through an approved-egress proxy.
detection_engineering: Alert on any ML dependency matching a known-CVE version range.
Reconstruct the AI dependency graph and alert on edges reaching external providers.
Annotate findings with regulatory classification for EMEA POVs.

references.title: Unit42 — ML Supply Chain Compromise Patterns
MITRE ATT&CK T1195.002 — Compromise Software Supply Chain

changelog.author: Henry Reed
changelog.change: Authored real AI-SPM supply-chain XQL against cortex_cloud_posture / ai_spm_findings, added correlation CR-AISPM-0003, enriched identity + panw_mapping. Promoted to active.
